# ParseFS called with an fs.FS function parameter that originates from
# a package-level embed.FS in another package. Template type errors should
# still be caught through the parameter chain.

! check-templates ./...
stderr 'BadField'

-- go.mod --
module example.com/app

go 1.25.0
-- assets.go --
package app

import "embed"

//go:embed templates/*.gohtml
var TemplatesFS embed.FS
-- web/web.go --
package web

import (
	"fmt"
	"html/template"
	"io/fs"
	"net/http"
)

type Page struct {
	Title string
}

func New(fsys fs.FS) http.Handler {
	ts := template.Must(template.New("").ParseFS(fsys, "templates/*.gohtml"))
	mux := http.NewServeMux()
	mux.HandleFunc("/", func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
		_ = ts.ExecuteTemplate(w, "index.gohtml", Page{Title: "Home"})
	})
	return mux
}

var _ = fmt.Sprint
-- cmd/server/main.go --
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"net/http"

	app "example.com/app"
	"example.com/app/web"
)

func main() {
	handler := web.New(app.TemplatesFS)
	_ = http.ListenAndServe(":8080", handler)
}

var _ = fmt.Sprint
-- templates/index.gohtml --
<h1>{{.BadField}}</h1>
